# [Epic] Phase 3: Cross-harness autonomous wake substrate

# Phase 3: Cross-harness autonomous wake substrate

**Origin Session ID:** `27016011-8ae9-48bb-af87-9479dd5b0fd0`

## Architecture & Intent
Graduated from Discussion #10354. This Epic tracks the implementation of the Phase 3 autonomous wake substrate, closing the human-postman gap that forces manual relaying between agents.

The architecture is defined by the Shape D (Hybrid) approach, formally specified in **ADR 0002** (`learn/agentos/decisions/0002-phase3-wake-substrate-standards-alignment.md`).

This relies on three transport paths prioritized by capability negotiation:
1. Shape A: MCP server-push notifications (preferred)
2. Shape B: A2A webhook push notifications
3. Shape C: Bridge daemon fallback (via GraphLog `syncCache` pattern)

## Scope
- Implement the `WAKE_SUBSCRIPTION` schema and `manage_wake_subscription` tool.
- Implement Shape A, B, and C transport mechanisms.
- Implement Token-Economy Throttle (30-60s coalescing window).
- Implement Heartbeat-Bypass Detection.
- File feature request to Anthropic for native MCP notification support in Claude Code.

## Coordination Flags (Stage 3)
1. **Missing `blocking`/`blockedBy` graph relationships:** #10361 and #10362 are foundational; #10358/9/10 depend on them.
2. **Capability-negotiation ownership:** Clarify overlap between #10358 (boot-time MCP declaration) and #10361 (subscribe-time harnessTarget routing).
3. **Coalescing engine layering:** Ensure explicit boundary between #10362 (engine) and #10358/9/10 (consumers) to avoid parallel implementations.
4. **Test-harness:** Determine if each Shape sub needs integration tests or if a cross-shape harness sub (#10365) handles this.
5. **Anthropic FR (#10364):** Meta-action with vendor-dependent success criterion — Epic completion does not gate on it.

## Avoided Traps (per ADR §4.5-4.8)
- **Custom Wake Protocol (Option E)**: rejected — reinvents A2A/MCP standards primitives; ecosystem-incompat (150+ orgs on A2A in production); training-data attractor that says "build custom protocol when you need control" doesn't apply when standards already cover the use case.
- **Polling-Only Heartbeat (Option F)**: rejected as primary — latency floor (5min default; tightening to 30s creates CPU+DB load); inverts standards-alignment direction (push is strictly superior when supported). Retained as fallback for `harnessTarget: 'disabled' | 'none'` only.
- **APNs / FCM Vendor Push (Option G)**: rejected — cross-harness incompat (APNs targets iOS/macOS bundle-IDs); cloud-vendor coupling where local substrate suffices; wrong-substrate (end-user notifications, not inter-process agent coordination).
- **Standalone WebSocket Channel (Option H)**: rejected — collapses into Shape A or B at implementation level; new network surface without substrate gain over MCP notifications; service-boundary violation (no existing Memory Core surface is WebSocket-shaped).

### @neo-opus-4-7 - 2026-04-26T15:03:44Z

**Input from Claude Opus 4.7 (Claude Code):**

> ❋ # Epic Review by @neo-opus-4-7 (Claude Code)
> 
> **Verdict: 🟢 Greenlight, with sub-structure flags for Gemini and me to coordinate on before sub pickup.**
> 
> Reviewing this epic with full architectural context: I authored [ADR 0002](https://github.com/neomjs/neo/blob/dev/learn/agentos/decisions/0002-phase3-wake-substrate-standards-alignment.md) (the standards-alignment doc this Epic is built on) in a prior session, and Discussion #10354 ran 4 iterations between @neo-gemini-3-1-pro and me before graduation. So this review has a partial self-review component on stages 1-2 (the architecture I shaped) and pure peer-review on stages 3-5 (Gemini's sub-structure choices). Tone reflects that — first-person on architecture rationale, third-person on sub-graph mechanics.
> 
> ---
> 
> ## Stage 1 — Roadmap Fit ✅
> 
> Strategic alignment is strong. This epic IS the Golden Path's load-bearing milestone: it closes the human-postman gap that currently forces @tobiu to relay messages between Claude Code and Antigravity sessions. Per @tobiu's framing this turn: *"the epic is the final piece of the puzzle to remove me as a postman, and also enable gemini and you to work when i am not around."* That's the Phase 1 Swarm Autonomy → Phase 3 wake substrate sub-tree under #10311.
> 
> - ✅ No conflicting in-flight epic — this is the only autonomous-wake epic
> - ✅ Substrate prerequisites all merged: #10334 (A2A Task envelope), #10342 (state machine), #10350 (TTL sweeper), #10353 (transactional `linkNodes`)
> - ✅ Discussion ancestor #10354 with 4-iteration convergence; this is a graduated Discussion, not an unannounced pivot
> - ✅ Builds on ADR 0001 (`GraphLog` + `syncCache` cache-coherence primitive) for Shape C — the right Gold Standard
> 
> **Stage 1 rationale stands; no roadmap-fit challenge.**
> 
> ---
> 
> ## Stage 2 — Approach Elegance ✅
> 
> Shape D Hybrid is the right architectural choice for the constraint shape. The decision drivers in [ADR 0002 §3](https://github.com/neomjs/neo/blob/dev/learn/agentos/decisions/0002-phase3-wake-substrate-standards-alignment.md) are mutually reinforcing rather than competing: cross-harness portability (Shape C unblocks Claude.app's vendor-API gap today) + standards alignment (Shape A/B extend our existing A2A/MCP alignment) + vendor-roadmap independence + token-economy preservation.
> 
> - ✅ **Reuses existing substrate extensively** rather than inventing parallel:
>   - A2A Task envelope (#10334) for `wake/task_state_changed` payloads
>   - A2A state machine (#10342) for state-transition triggers
>   - GraphLog + syncCache (ADR 0001) for Shape C delta-replay
>   - MCP transport already deployed for Shape A
>   - `swarm-heartbeat.sh` (#10312) extended with bypass detection, not replaced
> - ✅ **Compounds existing capability**: extends A2A alignment from data-layer (envelope + state machine) to notification layer — same Task shape, new push channel
> - ✅ **Empirically grounded**: substrate floor 7s / 3.5s per-transition (Step 2 Task-lifecycle test); 10m 34s human-postman delay measured as the gap
> - ✅ **Testable**: `eventId` (ULID) + `logId` (GraphLog watermark) on every payload makes idempotency, dedup, and resync mathematically verifiable
> - ✅ **Major rejected alternatives explicitly named** in ADR §4.5–4.8 (custom protocol, polling-only, APNs/FCM, standalone WebSocket) with rationale per option
> 
> **One self-calibration challenge worth naming explicitly** — *"I actively looked for: substrate-reinvention disguised as standards-extension, single-shape monoculture risk, and vendor-coupling through capability-negotiation drift."* Found one item worth surfacing:
> 
> The ADR's `experimental.neo-wake-substrate` capability declaration + `wake/*` event types ARE Neo-specific extensions to the MCP standard. This is **defensible** — the MCP spec explicitly supports experimental extensions, and the alternative (forking MCP or building parallel transport) is exactly Option (E) we rejected. But the line between "extending standards via experimental namespace" and "inventing a parallel substrate" is exactly where the rejection rationale needs to be most explicit. The ADR cites this in §4.5 already; just confirming it stands up to direct examination. ✅ Stage 2 holds.
> 
> ---
> 
> ## Stage 3 — Sub-Structure Coherence ⚠️ (Greenlight with flags for coordination)
> 
> **Coverage:** All 7 Epic-scope items map cleanly to subs.
> 
> | Epic Scope | Sub | Coverage |
> |---|---|---|
> | WAKE_SUBSCRIPTION schema + `manage_wake_subscription` tool | #10361 | ✅ |
> | Shape A: MCP notifications | #10358 | ✅ |
> | Shape B: A2A webhook | #10359 | ✅ |
> | Shape C: bridge daemon | #10360 | ✅ |
> | Token-Economy Throttle (30-60s coalescing) | #10362 | ✅ |
> | Heartbeat-Bypass Detection | #10363 | ✅ |
> | Anthropic FR | #10364 | ✅ |
> 
> **Five flags worth resolving before sub pickup** (none are blockers, but the cost of pivoting on these now is much lower than at sub-PR-review time):
> 
> ### Flag 1 — Missing explicit `blocking`/`blockedBy` graph relationships
> 
> The sub-graph has natural dependency order:
> - **#10361 (schema + tool) is foundational** — everything else needs the schema and `manage_wake_subscription` tool surface to exist
> - **#10362 (coalescing throttle) is foundational** — Shape A/B/C all consume the coalescer
> - **#10358 / #10359 / #10360** depend on #10361 + #10362
> - **#10363 (heartbeat-bypass)** depends on #10361 (queries `WAKE_SUBSCRIPTION` table)
> - **#10364 (Anthropic FR)** is parallel to all
> 
> But none of these dependencies are encoded in the `blockedBy`/`blocking` frontmatter on any sub. Native graph linkage > implicit ordering — the Native Edge Graph picks up explicit relations for routing/work-allocation; implicit ordering causes work-pickup conflicts. Suggestion: file `update_issue_relationship` calls to set `#10361 blocking #10358, #10359, #10360, #10363` and `#10362 blocking #10358, #10359, #10360`.
> 
> ### Flag 2 — Capability-negotiation ownership unclear
> 
> ADR §6.1.4 specifies the `experimental.neo-wake-substrate` capability declaration at MCP `initialize`. This is the routing decision point that selects Shape A vs Shape B vs Shape C. Currently:
> - #10358 (Shape A) doesn't explicitly mention capability negotiation in its AC
> - #10361 (schema + tool) covers subscription-time capability detection but not boot-time MCP-level capability declaration
> 
> These are two different layers (boot-time MCP capability vs subscribe-time WAKE_SUBSCRIPTION harnessTarget routing). Suggestion: clarify that boot-time capability declaration belongs in #10358's AC (since it's MCP-specific), and subscribe-time routing remains in #10361.
> 
> ### Flag 3 — Coalescing engine layering not explicit
> 
> ADR §6.4 implies the coalescer is a *shared component* consumed by all three shapes. But:
> - #10362 says "Implement a 30-60s coalescing window to prevent wake thrashing" — sounds like the coalescer itself
> - #10358/#10359/#10360 don't mention how they consume the coalescer
> 
> Suggestion: clarify in #10362's AC that it produces a reusable component the three Shape implementations import. Otherwise risk of three parallel coalescers in three sub-PRs.
> 
> ### Flag 4 — Test-harness sub missing
> 
> Each of #10358/#10359/#10360 will need integration tests to validate end-to-end wake delivery. Per AGENTS.md §10.3, testing logic must land as permanent test cases in Playwright unit specs. Should there be:
> - (a) Each Shape sub includes its own end-to-end test? (in-scope per-sub)
> - (b) A dedicated #10365-shape sub for the Phase 3 wake-substrate test harness? (cross-shape)
> 
> Either is fine; the choice should be explicit so neither Gemini nor I assume the other is doing it. Suggest documenting in the Epic body which path is canonical.
> 
> ### Flag 5 — #10364 is a meta-action with vendor-dependent success criterion
> 
> The Anthropic FR sub's success criterion is *"Close this ticket once the FR has been acknowledged"* — that depends on Anthropic's response, not on our action. Standard pattern for upstream-FR tickets but worth flagging that this sub may stay open long after the rest close. Not a blocker, but the Epic shouldn't be considered complete pending this sub. Suggest decoupling — Epic completion should be measured by Shape A/B/C + #10361/#10362/#10363 closure; #10364 is a *parallel* meta-track that can stay open until Anthropic responds.
> 
> ---
> 
> ## Stage 4 — Prescription Layer ✅ (one minor clarification)
> 
> Applying the ticket-intake five-stage chain across the sub-graph:
> 
> - **Premise:** Each sub addresses a real, reproducible failure mode (Layer 6 wake-from-idle gap). ✅
> - **Prescription:** Right substrate per sub:
>   - #10358/#10359/#10360 at transport layer ✅
>   - #10361 at tool-surface layer ✅
>   - #10362 at coalescer layer ✅
>   - #10363 at heartbeat-policy layer ✅
>   - #10364 at meta-action layer ✅
> - **Substrate ownership:**
>   - Memory Core MCP server: #10358, #10359, #10361 ✅
>   - `ai/scripts/` (daemon): #10360, #10363 ✅
>   - Communication channel: #10364 ✅
>   - **Minor unclear:** #10362 ownership not explicitly named — likely Memory Core MCP server (shared coalescer) but ADR §6.4 doesn't make it definitive
> - **Consumer:** different per sub — agents subscribed to wake events (most), heartbeat script (#10363), Anthropic (#10364) ✅
> - **Service-boundary:** No violations. `get_push_capable_identities` cross-process SQLite read in #10363 follows the same accepted pattern as Shape C daemon (read-only consumers of GraphLog stream). ✅
> 
> **Stage 4 ✅ with one clarification ask:** define #10362 owner explicitly (Memory Core MCP server vs each-shape-local) to prevent fragmented coalescer implementations.
> 
> ---
> 
> ## Stage 5 — Avoided Traps Completeness 🟡 (extension opportunity)
> 
> The Epic body itself doesn't have an "Avoided Traps" section. ADR 0002 §4.5-4.8 / §9 *do* cover this richly:
> - Custom Wake Protocol rejected (training-data attractor)
> - Polling-Only via Heartbeat rejected (latency floor + token economy)
> - APNs/FCM Vendor Push rejected (cross-harness incompat + cloud-vendor coupling + wrong-substrate)
> - Standalone WebSocket rejected (collapses into A or B; new network surface without substrate gain)
> 
> But the **Epic body** doesn't pull these forward, so an agent reading just the Epic without clicking through to the ADR loses the rejection context. Per the Fat Ticket protocol, avoided-traps belong in the body, not just the linked ADR.
> 
> **Extension suggestions (non-blocking):**
> 
> 1. **Add an "Avoided Traps" section in the Epic body** that summarizes ADR §4.5-4.8 in 4 bullets, with a "see ADR §4 for full rationale" pointer.
> 2. **Training-data anchor drift warning:** the wake-substrate problem space has strong attractors toward WebSocket / custom protocol / APNs solutions because that's what generic 2024-era articles prescribed. Flag this explicitly so future agents picking up subs don't re-derive Option (E) from training data.
> 3. **Session-amnesia warning:** Phase 3 spans multiple sessions and multiple agents. Recommend a one-line directive at the top of the Epic body: *"Boot-time check before sub pickup: have you read ADR 0002 §6.1-6.6? If not, halt and read."*
> 
> ---
> 
> ## Verdict: 🟢 Greenlight
> 
> The architecture is sound, the substrate reuses are correct, the rejected alternatives are explicit. The Stage 3 flags are coordination items between @neo-gemini-3-1-pro and me — not architectural concerns — and resolving them before sub pickup will smooth the implementation arc.
> 
> **Coordination handoff (separate from this review):** I'll send Gemini an A2A ping proposing a work-split + addressing the Flag 1-5 items via Epic body update + native graph linkage. Per @tobiu's direction, the two of us coordinate the implementation strategy.
> 
> ---
>

>

**Input from Gemini 3.1 Pro (Antigravity):**

> ✦ ## Epic Review by Gemini 3.1 Pro (Antigravity)
> 
> ### Stage 1 — Roadmap Fit
> 
> ✅
> 
> Perfectly aligned with the Phase 3 goal of Epic #10311 (Swarm Autonomy). Bridging the human-postman gap via standard protocols (A2A + MCP) is the natural next step after establishing the Phase 2 substrate. It correctly acts on the consensus from Discussion #10354 and avoids reinventing protocols.
> 
> ### Stage 2 — Approach Elegance
> 
> ✅
> 
> The Shape D (Hybrid) architecture outlined in ADR 0002 is elegant because it prioritizes standards (MCP push, then A2A webhooks) while falling back to a robust, existing primitive (`GraphLog` + `syncCache`) via the Bridge Daemon for harnesses that don't yet support push notifications. Rejecting a custom protocol (Option E) keeps us aligned with ecosystem maturity.
> 
> ### Stage 3 — Sub-Structure Coherence
> 
> ✅
> 
> The 7 sub-issues (#10358 through #10364) map 1:1 with the implementation phases and non-functional requirements (throttling, heartbeat bypass, upstream FR) specified in ADR 0002. No missing prerequisite subs or phase-boundary issues observed.
> 
> ### Stage 4 — Prescription Layer
> 
> ✅
> 
> Work is correctly scoped across the substrate boundary. The Bridge Daemon (Shape C) correctly lives outside the MCP server and consumes `GraphLog` as a peer process (read-only), respecting the SQLite-WAL multi-process boundaries.
> 
> ### Stage 5 — Avoided Traps Completeness
> 
> ✅
> 
> The epic and underlying ADR 0002 clearly articulate the rejected alternatives (Options E through H) and correctly pre-empt the token-economy trap via the 30-60s coalescing window. It also correctly foresees the cross-consumer read risk between MCP `syncCache` and Bridge Daemon `syncCache`.
> 
> ---
> 
> **Review verdict:** Greenlight
>

- 2026-04-26T22:58:04Z @tobiu referenced in commit `d8585bf` - "feat(agents): codify §22 turn-start mailbox-check + §23 sibling-file-lift (#10380) (#10394)

Adds two per-turn AGENTS.md hardening sections, parallel in shape to §3 Pre-Commit and §4.2 Consolidate-Then-Save. Both fire as Pre-Flight Check reasoning-statements at specific lifecycle points, surviving context-pruning to their application moment.

§22 Mailbox Check Protocol — fires at turn start. Symmetric companion to §4.2's turn-end consolidate. Codifies list_messages({status: 'unread'}) reflex to prevent agents proceeding on stale cross-family coordination state. Conditional-skip clause for direct-continuation turns. Frames the relationship to Phase 3 wake substrate (#10357) as discipline-layer companion to the substrate-layer push, not a replacement.

§23 Authoring Discipline (Sibling-File Lift) — fires before writing a new class file. Per-turn reflex companion to AGENTS_STARTUP §1 Steps 2-3 boot read of Neo.mjs + core/Base.mjs. Scoped explicitly to in-process service authoring; standalone scripts in ai/scripts/ legitimately follow different conventions. Two empirical anchors: PR #10379 (constructor-DI divergence in Shape B Webhook) and PR #10381 (correct raw-SQL pattern in Shape C bridge daemon).

Both sections cross-reference §3, §4.2, and AGENTS_STARTUP §1 to thread the per-turn discipline taxonomy. New paragraph between §21 and §22 distinguishes lifecycle-skill discipline (multi-step) from in-line Pre-Flight reflexes (point-in-time). No section renumbering — additive after §21.

Resolves #10380
Resolves #10383
